channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Amit Shah backs fast-track courts as Centre promises strict action in NEET paper leak cases
item.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah said that protecting the welfare and future of India's youth remains the Modi government's highest priority, while expressing support for Prime Minister Narendra Modi's d...

item.title: Global passport rankings reveal widening differences in international travel freedom
item.description: The article examines how the strength of passports varies across the world and what these differences reveal about global inequality, diplomacy and changing geopolitical relationships. Passport streng...

item.title: Niger's Military Junta Intensifies Human Rights Abuses
item.description: The article details how Niger's military junta, which seized power in 2023, has entrenched authoritarianism by dismantling democratic institutions and escalating repression. Key actions include dissol...

item.title: Kenyans Face Higher Fuel and Electricity Costs as Global Oil Prices Surge Past KSh 14k a Barrel
item.description: Global oil prices have surged past KSh 14,500 per barrel, causing Kenyan consumers to face higher fuel and electricity costs. Murban crude, Kenya's benchmark for oil imports, rose over 22% to $112 per...

item.title: International Oil Companies Shift Focus to Nigeria's Deepwater Resources
item.description: International oil companies (IOCs) are increasingly investing in Nigeria's deep offshore oil reserves, marking a strategic shift after divesting onshore and shallow-water assets. This move is driven b...

item.title: Canadian Authorities Disrupt Extremist Threats Against Jewish Communities
item.description: A federal government report obtained by Global News reveals that Canadian authorities have thwarted four extremist plots targeting Jewish communities since the start of the Israel-Hamas conflict in 20...

item.title: Angela Ken's First Solo Concert: Highlights and Expectations
item.description: Angela Ken, the popular Filipino singer and actress, is set to make her mark with her first solo concert, marking a significant milestone in her career. The event, scheduled to take place at the New F...

item.title: ACT Deputy Chief Minister Yvette Berry resigns from cabinet following Integrity Commission findings
item.description: ACT Deputy Chief Minister Yvette Berry has resigned from the ACT cabinet following the release of an ACT Integrity Commission report that found her former chief of staff and the former head of the Edu...

item.title: 15 Religious Leaders Under SIU Investigation for Alleged Fraudulent Immigration Benefits
item.description: Minister of Home Affairs Dr Leon Schreiber confirmed that the Special Investigating Unit (SIU) has identified 15 religious leaders, including pastors and prophets, for investigation into alleged fraud...

item.title: Niger Journalists Freed After Eight Months in Jail, One Still Detained
item.description: Two Nigerian journalists, Youssouf Seriba and Oumarou Kané, have been released from Kollo Prison after eight months of detention, according to a press release by the Media Foundation for West Africa (...

item.title: Canadian premiers address Trump tariffs and prioritize health care funding
item.description: Canadian provincial premiers convened to discuss strategies against the potential economic impact of U.S. tariffs, with a focus on health care funding and energy policy. The meeting highlighted concer...

item.title: EFF's 2026 Local Government Manifesto Focuses on Municipal Rebuilding, Jobs, and Dignity
item.description: The article discusses the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) manifesto for the 2026 local government elections, emphasizing their commitment to transforming municipalities into developmental institutions...

item.title: LHRC Condemns Prolonged Detention of 55 Bailable Offenders
item.description: The Legal and Human Rights Centre (LHRC) has strongly criticized the prolonged detention of at least 55 individuals accused of bailable offences, urging police to release them immediately and uphold l...

item.title: Nairobi Senator Returns Official Vehicle After ODM Removal
item.description: Nairobi Senator Edwin Sifuna has handed back the official Toyota Land Cruiser Prado that was allegedly gifted to him by ODM leader Raila Odinga. The vehicle, which was assigned to him during his tenur...

item.title: Philippines asserts sovereignty over disputed waters amid China tensions
item.description: Gilberto Teodoro Jr., a prominent Filipino official, recently emphasized the Philippines' firm stance on its territorial claims in the West Philippine Sea, particularly around the Ayungin Shoal. The s...

item.title: South Gauteng DPP Andrew Chauke Cleared of Misconduct Allegations
item.description: Advocate Andrew Chauke, the South Gauteng Director of Public Prosecutions, has been cleared of all misconduct allegations following the Nkabinde Commission of Inquiry's final report. The independent p...

item.title: Reader's Question on Poor Judgment and Seeking Advice
item.description: The article discusses a weekly feature where readers submit advice for Carolyn Hax's unanswered questions, highlighting the importance of thoughtful guidance. It emphasizes the role of advice columnis...

item.title: Drought warnings persist as UK braces for weekend rainfall
item.description: The UK is facing severe drought conditions in several regions, with hosepipe bans already in place across southern and central England. Since mid-June, many areas have experienced significantly below-...

item.title: MAGA's $30M Funding Fuels Democratic Primary Battle Against Progressive Rival
item.description: The article examines the significant financial influence of MAGA-aligned donors in a pivotal Democratic Senate race in Michigan, where $30 million in funding is being used to support Haley Stevens aga...

item.title: TMC MP suspended from Lok Sabha over alleged misconduct and party merger tensions
item.description: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee was suspended from the Lok Sabha for the remainder of the monsoon session after allegations of using inappropriate language towards women members of the Nationali...
